objectives: Explain core concepts of database management systems
Operate relational database management system for basic administrative tasks
Compare MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server at a high level
learning_path.title: Relational Database Foundations and DBMS Operations

objectives: Create conceptual and logical data models aligned to requirements
Design normalized schemas and define relationships
Translate models into implementable database design
learning_path.title: Data Modeling and Schema Design for Relational Databases

objectives: Choose appropriate indexing strategies for common access patterns
Analyze query behavior and identify optimization opportunities
Apply indexing and query optimization techniques to improve performance
learning_path.title: Indexing and Query Optimization Essentials

objectives: Implement least-privilege access patterns for users and roles
Configure authentication/authorization approaches within a relational database
Audit and harden access pathways to reduce risk
learning_path.title: Database Security and Access Control

objectives: Define backup objectives and recovery targets
Execute backup workflows and validate restore procedures
Design recovery runbooks for common failure scenarios
learning_path.title: Backup and Recovery Planning and Execution

objectives: Differentiate high availability from disaster recovery requirements
Design HADR approaches aligned to recovery objectives
Validate failover and recovery procedures through controlled testing
learning_path.title: High Availability and Disaster Recovery (HADR) Strategies

objectives: Explain replication goals and common topologies
Configure and operate replication workflows for continuity and scale
Troubleshoot replication lag and consistency issues
learning_path.title: Database Replication Concepts and Operations

objectives: Identify key health and performance signals to monitor
Set up alerting thresholds and escalation paths
Use monitoring data to drive operational improvements
learning_path.title: Database Monitoring and Alerting for Reliability

objectives: Perform essential Linux/Unix tasks that support database services
Manage processes, storage, and permissions relevant to database reliability
Apply operational practices that reduce downtime and improve maintainability
learning_path.title: Linux/Unix Administration for Database Operations

objectives: Perform core operational tasks across MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server
Compare operational differences that affect performance, security, and recovery
Apply consistent operational practices across multiple database platforms
learning_path.title: Platform Deep Dive: MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server Operations

matched_occupation.description: Database administrators test, implement and administer computer databases. They use their expertise in database management systems to plan, coordinate and implement security measures to safeguard computer databases. They also use scripts and configuration files to tailor a database to users' needs.
matched_occupation.label: database administrator
